concentration.headline: Moderately concentrated — one leading city
concentration.body: Bogotá leads Colombia's urban system with 15.9% of tracked urban population across 732 cities — significant but not overwhelming. Secondary cities retain meaningful economic weight alongside the capital.
concentration.comparison: Similar to: United Kingdom, Italy, Canada
concentration.contrast: Unlike: USA, Japan (more distributed) or Thailand, Argentina (more dominant)

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Barranquilla & Soledad
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Barranquilla and Soledad (9 km apart, combined population 2,002,602). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 9 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Colombia sits between distributed and dominant: Bogotá leads with 15.9% but doesn't overwhelm. Gravity clusters tightly around Barranquilla → Soledad (9 km avg) — a strong sub-regional core coexists with the national leader, creating a layered urban hierarchy.
